key_insight: This study identifies exhaustion-like bone marrow T-cell islands (eBM-TIs) as a spatial immune niche that expands with multiple myeloma progression and is linked to chronic inflammatory signaling.
clinical_relevance: The proportion of eBM-TI-like T cells was associated with higher disease risk and worse prognostic outcomes, suggesting potential value as a spatial biomarker for myeloma risk stratification.
method: Analyzed Xenium 5K bone marrow spatial transcriptomics from control, MGUS, smoldering myeloma, and multiple myeloma samples.
Used Gaussian mixture model (GMM) spatial partitioning plus multi-sample integration and cell-type annotation to define T-cell niches.
Applied multilayer perceptron (MLP) transfer to scRNA-seq cohorts with clinical staging information.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that exhaustion-like T cells increased across disease progression, formed spatially discrete islands, and showed IFN-related and CXCR3 ligand (CXCL9/CXCL10) signaling linked to clinical risk.
watch_item: The findings are based on spatial transcriptomics and computational label transfer, so independent validation in larger patient cohorts is needed before clinical use.
paper_title: Spatial transcriptomics and machine learning define exhaustion-like bone marrow T-cell islands associated with myeloma progression and clinical risk
